description: Context-continuity bucket, enacted: a mid-build increment stays on the current primitive without re-classifying. The exam's ctx-inline cases fake prior context with synthetic priorConversation text; here the prior workflow is actually built in turn 1, so there is no byte-identical exam sibling (documented exemption in the contract test). Doubles as the over-trigger guard: the intent gate must fire once for the new automation and stay quiet for the edit. Calibrated at N=3 (2026-07-08): gate-once passed 3/3; one iteration flipped the edit expectation on an artifact-visibility race (proxy asked before the build registered), mitigated by the wait-for-confirmation director wording.

conversation.text: Every weekday at 9am, Slack me the Berlin weather forecast.

text: [Wait until the agent has confirmed the workflow was created — it will say so explicitly. Only then ask for exactly one change: make it 8:30 instead of 9am. Approve any reasonable plan or setup card. End the conversation once the change is applied.]

processExpectations: The agent calls the load_skill tool with the intent-recognition skill at most once in the whole conversation, before the initial build — and does not load it again for the follow-up schedule change.
The agent treats the 8:30 request as an edit to the existing workflow rather than classifying or building a new automation.
